name: media-server
short_description: High-performance HLS media server with smart transcoding and remuxing for video and audio streaming.
long_description: Media Server is a FastAPI-based HTTP Live Streaming (HLS) platform that handles universal video and audio format support with intelligent processing. It automatically detects whether files can be remuxed as-is (zero-cost operation for H.264+AAC) or require transcoding, then produces HLS-compatible segments for streaming. The system uses SQLite with Peewee ORM for persistent job queue management, implements a background worker thread for transcoding with graceful shutdown, and provides a web player interface with progress tracking and job cancellation. It includes path traversal protection, rate limiting, structured logging, and configurable retention policies for automatic cleanup.

modules.description: FastAPI web server with REST API endpoints for streaming, job management, health checks, and static file serving with path traversal protection and rate limiting.
Background worker that polls the SQLite job queue, executes FFmpeg transcoding/remuxing processes, tracks progress, handles graceful shutdown, and supports job cancellation.
Job management layer that auto-detects whether files can be remuxed or must be transcoded, maintains in-memory progress cache for responsive UI, and provides database persistence for job metadata.
Peewee ORM models defining the Job table and database operations including job claiming, stale job recovery, cleanup of old jobs, and queue statistics.
Centralized runtime configuration singleton with environment variable overrides for paths, worker concurrency, segment duration, job retention, and rate limiting.
Main web player interface using Video.js for HLS playback with file selection and real-time transcoding progress display.
Client-side JavaScript handling file listing, job submission, progress polling, player control, and cancel functionality.
Embeddable widget script for integrating the HLS player into external websites.
Demo page showcasing the embeddable player widget capabilities.

author: Philip Orange <git@philiporange.com>
